title: 10 best herbal decoctions for gum swelling
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for gum swelling are natural remedies made by steeping herbs in hot water to create a medicinal liquid that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with swollen gums. These decoctions work by releasing active compounds that combat bacterial infections, ease pain, and promote healing. Examples of effective herbal decoctions include those made from chamomile, calendula, and peppermint, which have been used for centuries to soothe gum swelling and improve oral health. By using these decoctions, individuals can enjoy relief from discomfort and reduce the risk of more serious complications, ultimately improving their overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and puffiness. The extract from the licorice root contains compounds like glycyrrhizin, which inhibits the production of prostaglandins, a hormone-like substance that causes inflammation in the mouth. This natural remedy also has antiseptic properties, which help to combat bacteria and viruses that can contribute to gum swelling. As a result, herbal licorice decoctions can provide relief from swollen gums and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as glycyrrhizin and glabridin, which help to reduce inflammation in the gums and alleviate swelling.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in licorice root decoctions help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to gum inflammation and damage, promoting a healthy gum environment.

Soothing and protective properties: Licorice root has been shown to have a soothing effect on mucous membranes, including the gums, while also providing protection against bacterial and fungal infections.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thus reducing gum swelling.
Licopyrone A (Licorinic acid): Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in gum tissues, thereby alleviating swelling.
Glucuronic acid: Possesses anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help in reducing gum inflammation and pain, thus providing relief from swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which is responsible for the anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce gum swelling.
Leaves: The leaves contain smaller amounts of glycyrrhizin but are still used in decoctions to treat gum swelling due to their overall medicinal properties.
Barks: The barks are occasionally used in decoctions to treat gum swelling due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, although they contain less glycyrrhizin than the roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 to 60 grams of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra for decoction.
Chop the roots into smaller pieces to increase the surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped roots with 1 liter of water in a saucepan.
Boil the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids to obtain the final decoction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold decoctions helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and discomfort in the gums. The flavonoids present in pot marigold, such as luteolin and apigenin, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, pot marigold's antioxidant properties help to protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals, further contributing to its effectiveness in soothing gum swelling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold decoctions contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in pot marigold decoctions neutralize free radicals that can contribute to inflammation and tissue damage in the gums, promoting healing and reducing swelling.

Antimicrobial: Pot marigold decoctions have antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum swelling and inflammation, thereby reducing swelling and promoting healthy oral tissues.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: This flavonoid helps with gum swelling by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Saponins: These glycosides help with gum swelling by reducing inflammation and inhibiting bacterial growth, which can contribute to gum inflammation.
Beta-sitosterol: This phytosterol helps with gum swelling by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory eicosanoids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce swelling and prevent infection in the gums.
Leaves: They are used because of their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing, which can aid in treating gum swelling and promoting healthy gum tissue.
Roots: They are used due to their potential to reduce pain and inflammation, which can help alleviate discomfort and promote healing in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 fresh calendula officinalis flowers at dawn when dew is present to ensure optimal potency.
Rinse the harvested flowers with cool water to remove dirt and debris then gently pat dry.
Combine the dried flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container to create a decoction.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Store the decoction in an airtight glass container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce redness and pain in the gums. The antioxidants present in dandelion root and leaves also help to combat oxidative stress, a major contributor to gum inflammation. Additionally, dandelion's antimicrobial properties work to eliminate bacterial infections that can cause swollen gums, allowing for a healthy balance of oral flora. This natural remedy provides a soothing and effective solution for alleviating gum swelling and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion root contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as taraxasterol, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in dandelion decoctions, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to gum inflammation and swelling.

Digestive and immune system support: Dandelion root has been shown to have prebiotic properties, promoting a healthy gut microbiome, which is essential for maintaining overall oral health and preventing gum swelling.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: A triterpenoid saponin that exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce gum swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Taraxasterol acetate: A triterpene saponin that has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, contributing to the reduction of gum swelling and associated pain.

Flavonoids (including kaempferol and quercetin): Polyphenolic compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce gum swelling by scavenging free radicals and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale are used because they contain taraxasterol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling.
Leaves: The leaves of Taraxacum officinale are used because they contain taraxanthin, a triterpene ester with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Taraxacum officinale are used because they contain taraxasterol and taraxanthin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in the morning for optimal potency and flavor.
Chop the taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers into small pieces to release their essential oils and flavor compounds.
Combine 2 tablespoons of chopped taraxacum officinale with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the taraxacum officinale mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors and nutrients to infuse into the water.
Strain the taraxacum officinale decoction through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a clean container for consumption.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Lemon balm's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which contribute to gum swelling, by neutralizing free radicals and protecting against damage to gum tissue.

Ursolic acid: A triterpenoid that has potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help alleviate gum swelling by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the growth of bacteria that contribute to gum disease.

Linalool: A terpene that possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help mitigate gum swelling by reducing inflammation, preventing oxidative stress, and promoting healing in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melissa officinalis are commonly used to make decoctions for gum swelling due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots of Melissa officinalis are used to make decoctions for gum swelling as they contain compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.
Stems: The stems of Melissa officinalis are also used to make decoctions for gum swelling due to their ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of melissa officinalis leaves and flowers at peak bloom for optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the harvested melissa officinalis in a warm place for 24-48 hours or until completely dry and brittle.
Combine 5-10 grams of dried melissa officinalis with 1 liter of boiling water for a decoction.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes and then strain it through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and consume 20-30 milliliters as needed.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Flavonoids, phenolic acids, and lignans present in Kansas coneflower decoctions have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce gum swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Immunomodulatory effects: Kansas coneflower's immunomodulatory compounds may stimulate the immune system to produce cytokines that help regulate inflammation and tissue repair, which can aid in reducing gum swelling and promoting healing.

The plant's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with gum disease. The decoction's antibacterial properties also help combat the underlying bacterial infections that can cause gum swelling. Leaves: The leaves have been used to create decoctions that may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate gum swelling.
Stems: The stems of Echinacea angustifolia contain compounds that may help to soothe and reduce inflammation in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 dried echinacea angustifolia roots weighing 1-2 grams each and store them in an airtight container.
Heat 1 liter of water in a saucepan over medium heat until it reaches a rolling boil within 10-15 minutes.
Add the dried echinacea angustifolia roots to the boiling water and simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the echinacea angustifolia decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids within 2 minutes.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature for 30-60 minutes before refrigerating or consuming it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St john's wort decoctions helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains compounds that reduce inflammation and pain, making it an effective natural remedy for gum issues. When used as a decoction, the active ingredients in St john's wort are released into the liquid, allowing them to penetrate deep into the gums and provide relief from swelling and discomfort. This traditional remedy has been used for centuries to soothe irritated gums and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the gums by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins and other pro-inflammatory mediators.

Flavonoids: St John's Wort contains a range of flavonoids, including hyperoside and isoquercitrin, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that can help reduce gum inflammation and swelling.

Hypericin: This compound has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat periodontal pathogens that contribute to gum disease and swelling by reducing the severity of infections and promoting healing.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, helps reduce inflammation and swelling in gum tissues by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Hyperforin: Hyperforin, a phenolic compound, exhibits anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help alleviate gum swelling by reducing bacterial growth and inflammation.

Naphthodianthrones (e.g., Hypericin, Pseudohypericin): These compounds have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help mitigate gum swelling by neutralizing free radicals and reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their high flavonoid content, particularly hyperoside and quercetin, which exhibit anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce gum swelling.
Roots: Roots are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids, including hyperforin and adhyperforin, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that aid in gum swelling reduction.
Stems: Stems are used due to their high flavonoid content, including rutin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate gum swelling and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the aerial parts of hypericum perforatum when flowers are in full bloom and dry them thoroughly.
Measure out 10-20 grams of dried hypericum perforatum and place it in a saucepan.
Add 500 milliliters of water to the saucepan and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer the mixture for 5-10 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh myrrhis odorata leaves and roots from the wild or your garden.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm oven at 150°f for 1-2 hours.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried myrrhis odorata with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the decoction steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids before serving or storing in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey decoctions helps with gum swelling because of its unique composition, rich in allantoin, a potent anti-inflammatory agent. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of symphytum officinale when they are in their active growth stage.
Rinse the harvested leaves thoroughly with clean water to remove any dirt particles.
Combine 1 gram of dried symphytum officinale leaves with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a saucepan.
Steep the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried achillea millefolium flowers and leaves from a trusted source or grow your own.
Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried achillea millefolium flowers and leaves for every 8 ounces of water.
Combine the measured achillea millefolium flowers and leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5 to 10 minutes or until the desired strength is achieved.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup to remove solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried flowers of matricaria chamomilla and store them in a cool dry place.
Measure 1-2 teaspoons of the dried flowers for every 8 ounces of water.
Combine the measured flowers with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the flowers have lost their flavor and color.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried prunella vulgaris leaves and flowers.

remedy_recipe: Gather one ounce of dried eupatorium perfoliatum herbs and four cups of filtered water.

Simmer the decoction for an additional 30 minutes allowing the flavors to meld together.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried solidago virgaurea leaves and stems measuring one tablespoon for every 8 ounces of water.
Combine the herbal mixture with cold filtered water in a large pot or kettle.
Bring the liquid to a boil then reduce heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried foeniculum vulgare root powder into a clean glass cup.

Add the measured foeniculum vulgare root powder to the boiling water and stir well.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes, allowing the decoction to steep.

remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves and 400ml of distilled water in a clean glass container.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum swelling that are treated with herbal decoctions are various inflammatory conditions, including arthritis, rheumatism, and joint pain. Herbal decoctions like turmeric, ginger, and boswellia have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in these conditions. Additionally, decoctions made from herbs like calendula, chamomile, and peppermint can soothe oral mucositis and other mouth sores, providing relief similar to gum swelling.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with gum swelling is a blend of Calendula, Echinacea, and Sage. Calendula soothes and reduces inflammation, while Echinacea helps to boost the immune system and fight off infection. Sage, rich in antioxidants, helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing of the gums. Combine equal parts of each decoction and drink 2-3 times a day to help alleviate gum swelling and promote overall oral health. This combination may also help prevent future gum issues.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Iranian journal of basic medical sciences, herbal decoctions are good for gum swelling as they can act via several mechanisms such as decrease in gingival inflammation and bleeding, inhibition of dental plaque formation, and improvement in different indices of oral hygiene. The study found that various herbal products, including pomegranate, aloe, green tea, and miswak, were effective in treating gingivitis through these mechanisms. Specifically, the study reported that 100% of the herbal medicines in the form of dentifrice, mouth rinse, gel, and gum assessed in gingivitis via specific indices such as plaque index, bleeding index, microbial count, and biomarkers of inflammation, showed a positive effect, suggesting that herbal decoctions can be a valuable treatment option for gum swelling.

Barks: Some traditional medicine practitioners use the barks of Zingiber officinale to make decoctions for gum swelling, although it's less commonly used than the rhyzomes and roots.
remedy_recipe: Select 1-2 teaspoons of dried root of zingiber officinale and rinse it under cold running water.
Combine the dried root with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan to create a decoction.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for 5-7 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before consuming it in 1-2 doses per day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale decoctions helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and pain. The plant's active compounds, such as allantoin and mucilage, create a protective barrier around the affected area, reducing inflammation and allowing the gums to heal. Additionally, the decoction's soothing effects calm irritated tissues, promoting a return to normal gum function and reducing the risk of further irritation. This makes it a popular natural remedy for gum inflammation and swelling.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: They help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums by blocking the release of histamine and other chemical mediators.
Saponins: They possess anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums, promoting healing and tissue repair.
Flavonoids: They exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, promoting healthy tissue repair and regeneration.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Symphytum officinale are commonly used to make decoctions for gum swelling due to their high content of mucilage, a soothing and protective substance.
Roots: The roots are used to make decoctions for gum swelling because they contain a high concentration of mucilage and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh symphytum officinale roots in the spring or fall season for optimal potency.
Clean and chop the roots into small pieces to release their active compounds for easier extraction.
Combine the chopped roots with 2 cups of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes to extract the bioactive compounds from the roots.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids to obtain a concentrated herbal remedy for topical use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium decoctions helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and prevent infection in the gums. This leads to a decrease in swelling and discomfort. Additionally, Achillea millefolium decoctions have been traditionally used to promote oral health and reduce gum inflammation, making it a popular natural remedy for gum swelling and other oral health issues. Regular use may provide relief.
remedy_constituents: Essential oil: Helps with gum swelling by reducing inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties.
Flavonoids: Contributes to reducing gum swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Rosmarinic acid: Helps to alleviate gum swelling by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for decoctions due to their high content of sesquiterpene lactones, which help reduce inflammation and swelling.
Roots: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in alleviating gum swelling when used in decoctions.
Flowers: Employed for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce gum swelling and soothe the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh or dried achillea millefolium flowers and leaves from a trusted source.
Rinse the harvested plant material with cold water to remove any dirt or debris thoroughly.
Combine the rinsed plant material with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow the plant's active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Commiphora molmol decoctions helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce inflammation and discomfort in the gums. The active compounds in Commiphora molmol, such as ferulic acid and 8-epi-prostaglandin F2α, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby alleviating swollen and inflamed gums. Additionally, Commiphora molmol decoctions may also help to reduce bacterial growth and promote healing in the affected area, providing relief from gum swelling and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Boswellic acids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators.
Furofuran lignans: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in reducing gum inflammation and swelling by neutralizing free radicals and reducing oxidative stress.

Triterpenoids (especially ursolic acid and oleanolic acid): These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help in reducing gum swelling and preventing infection by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in myrrh resin, which is used to stimulate gum swelling.
Barks: They contain myrrh resin and other compounds that help to increase blood flow to the gums, promoting swelling.
Buds: They are a primary source of myrrh resin, which is often used in decoctions to stimulate gum swelling and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Measure 30 grams of dried commiphora molmol bark and add it to a medium-sized pot.
Combine 2 liters of water in the pot with the commiphora molmol bark and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the decoction steep for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth into a separate container and discard the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool down and store it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba decoctions helps with gum swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can help alleviate swollen gums. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's ability to improve blood flow and oxygenation of the tissues can promote healing and reduce redness and swelling in the gums. This can be particularly beneficial for people experiencing gum swelling due to gum disease or other oral health issues.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and swelling in gum tissues by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators.

Bilobalide: This triterpene derivative has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums by modulating the activity of immune cells and reducing the release of pro-inflammatory chemicals.
Quercetin: This flavonoid acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in gum tissues, thereby alleviating swelling and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their high flavonoid and terpenoid content, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for gum swelling.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are used in decoctions due to their high concentration of ginkgotoxin, which may have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate gum swelling.
Buds: The buds of Ginkgo biloba are used in decoctions because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that may help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with gum issues.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 1-2 teaspoons of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and place them in a heat-resistant container.
Boil 1 cup of water in a saucepan and reduce heat to a simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Add the ginkgo biloba leaves to the simmering water and let steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before refrigerating or consuming immediately.
